creator: ngie@FreeBSD.org
text:
mysql 5.7 - released 10/2015 ; EOL in 10/2023. Given the EOL date overlap, I think marking it broken/ignore on 3.x and do what's necessary to EOL that port seems sensible.

php 7.3 - EOL; should be marked broken/ignore.
php 7.4 - EOL; should be marked broken/ignore.
php 8.0 - EOL in 8 months. Depending on the required patch side, it should be marked broken/ignore or patched.

python 3.6 - has been EOLed since 12/2021. Marking broken/ignore seems sensible.

ruby 2.7 - EOL in just over 3 weeks; it should be marked broken/ignore.

Depending on whether or not the ports are officially listed as "EOL", the ports themselves should also be marked EOL (if this hasn't been done already).
